</imports>

<insights>

- High coupling (0.54) — 43 imports target "prd-schema-foundation"
- 10 entry points — wide API surface, consider consolidating exports
- Grouping CLI commands with the core lifecycle utilities they call (parent-completion.ts, timestamps.ts, parent-reset.ts) tightly couples presentation and domain logic — extracting the core utilities to the foundation zone would improve separation of concerns.
- The 43-import inflow from this zone to 'prd-schema-foundation' and 9-import inflow from core confirm this zone is a consumer tier sitting above the domain foundation, which is the correct dependency direction.
- cli/output.ts and cli/errors.ts as entry points suggest a shared CLI infrastructure layer is emerging — formalizing this as a dedicated output-infrastructure zone would make the presentation boundary explicit.
- Coupling of 0.54 exceeds the healthy midpoint, driven partly by core lifecycle utilities (parent-completion.ts, verify.ts, timestamps.ts) being grouped with command handlers — separating them would lower coupling and improve testability.
- Bidirectional imports between this zone and 'prd-schema-foundation' (43 in, 12 out) hint at a soft circular dependency; tracing the 12 outbound imports would confirm whether a true cycle exists or if they are legitimate downward calls.
- token-format.ts and usage.ts co-located with status and update commands reflects the CLI's role as the human-facing reporting layer — this is architecturally appropriate and should be preserved even if the zone is split.
- Zone "prd-cli-operations" has files across 6 directories — consider consolidating under a dedicated directory
- Both the prd-cli-operations zone (11 imports to store) and the cli zone (9 imports to store) independently access the store layer without a shared gateway, violating the CLAUDE.md gateway pattern. Two separate zones reaching into the same upstream package creates duplicated store-access logic and no single place to update when store APIs change.
- The cli zone (cohesion 0.22) receives 12 inbound imports from prd-cli-operations while also exporting to prd-schema-foundation (via constants.ts). This bidirectional position — imported by operations, importing from foundation — suggests cli is functioning as a shared output-infrastructure layer rather than a command zone, but without a formal interface contract.
- prd-cli-operations and the cli zone both import directly from the store layer (11 and 9 imports respectively) with no shared gateway. Per CLAUDE.md, all imports from a given upstream package should flow through a single gateway — creating a store gateway for CLI-tier consumers would make the store's public surface explicit and auditable.
- The cli zone (cohesion 0.22, 11 files) occupies a structural crossroads: it receives 12 imports from prd-cli-operations and exports shared utilities (output.ts, errors.ts) consumed by 7 zones. Its low cohesion reflects this mixed role. Formalizing it as an explicit CLI infrastructure layer with a defined public API would improve cohesion and remove the ambiguity that allows upward imports from foundation into it.
- src/cli/commands/adapter.ts manages store adapter configuration at runtime and directly imports from store/adapter-registry.ts without going through any gateway. This adds a fourth direct store access path from the CLI tier (joining prd-cli-operations' 11 other store imports and the cli zone's 9 store imports), further eroding the store boundary.
- src/cli/commands/adapter.ts imports store/adapter-registry.ts directly, bypassing the store gateway pattern mandated in CLAUDE.md. The adapter command is runtime infrastructure for configuring persistence — its store access should route through the same gateway as other CLI-tier consumers to prevent the store public API from fragmenting across four independent import paths.
- Three files — status.ts, status-shared.ts, status-sections.ts — represent informal decomposition of a single command that grew too large. The -sections and -shared suffixes signal internal splitting without zone-level separation, which means the three files still share the same zone boundary as unrelated command handlers (move, remove, update).
- token-format.ts and usage.ts are usage-reporting utilities co-located with CRUD command handlers (move, remove, update, add). These represent two distinct responsibilities — usage analytics formatting vs. item mutation commands — sharing a zone without a sub-boundary, which suppresses cohesion and obscures the zone's stated purpose.
- status.ts, status-shared.ts, and status-sections.ts form an implicit sub-zone for the status command. Create a src/cli/commands/status/ subdirectory and consolidate these three files there. This makes the informal decomposition explicit, improves discoverability, and creates a filesystem boundary that future zone analysis can detect.
- token-format.ts and usage.ts (usage reporting) are co-located with item-mutation commands (move, remove, update) in the same zone. Extract them to a src/cli/usage/ subdirectory. This separates read-only reporting from state-mutating commands and raises cohesion by removing the analytics responsibility from the CRUD command zone.
- [call graph] 1091 internal calls, 98 outgoing, 415 incoming (cohesion: 0.92, coupling: 0.08)

</insights>
